rulings.Case Title: MARK EMMETT WILSON VS. MIGUEL ALEJANDRO RESCALVO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Miguel Rescalvo, Det Norske Veritas As, Det Norske Veritas (U.S.A.) Inc. Motion For Summary Judgment Or Summary Adjudication; Appendix
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 19, 2014, Line 18, Defendant Miguel Rescalvo, Det Norske Veritas As, Det Norske Veritas (U.S.A.) Inc. Motion For Summary Judgment Or Summary Adjudication; Appendix. (Part 1 of 2) Motion for Summary Judgment granted. Defendants have shown Plaintiff was not entitled to commission payments after signing the Offer Letter. (Defendants' Statement of Undisputed Material Facts [DSUMF] #8.) Plaintiff has offered no evidence that rebuts that showing. Absent an allegation that the Offer Letter was unconscionable, Plaintiff's testimony as to Defendants' assurances is barred by the parole evidence rule. Also, the e-mail relied on by Plaintiff as establishing an agreement to continue commission payments does not clearly or persuasively indicate that the commissions referred to were due to or resulted from Plaintiff's efforts. (Plaintiff's Statement of Disputed Material Facts [PSDMF] #13.) As to the Addendum, Defendants' evidence establishes Plaintiff did not legally accept the offer therein, and therefore neither party is bound by its terms. As a condition of acceptance, Plaintiff was required to sign and return the Addendum to the Director of Operations and Human Resources, yet Plaintiff testified that he signed the Addendum and kept it at his desk. (PSDMF #22, 23.) Additionally, there is no reason to believe or interpret this requirement as merely a suggested method of acceptance. (C.f. Devencenzi v. Donkonics (1959) 170 Cal.App. 2d 513.) Plaintiff has also failed to establish that a triable issue of material fact exists as to intent to defraud. Rather, Plaintiff has produced evidence establishing at most the failure to perform a promise. (See Tenzer v. Superscope, Inc. (1985) 39 Cal.3d 18, 30-31.) Further, the evidence relied on by Plaintiff to circumstantially prove fraudulent intent is misconstrued and exaggerated, and does not establish insolvency, hasty repudiation, or continued assurances. (PSDMF #25.) In fact, Plaintiff's own evidence establishes that by "additional commissions," the Addendum was referring to commissions in addition to Plaintiff's salary. (PSDMF #25.) Moreover, because there are no triable issues as to whether Plaintiff was owed any commissions, Plaintiff as a matter of law will be unable to prove that he was terminated in violation of public policy. Next, Defendants have asserted and Plaintiff does not dispute that the allegedly defamatory statements are presumptively privileged, and Plaintiff has failed to show that the statements were made with malice. Defendant Rescalvo's testimony was corroborated by another employee, which provides a reasonable ground for belief in Rescalvo's statements. (DSUMF #338-40.) As to misclassification, Plaintiff has failed to identify any recoverable damages pursuant to Labor Code § 227.3, or identify any other statutory provision providing for recovery of 401(k) and "other benefits." As to the breach of contract claim, Plaintiff relies on execution of the Addendum for the implied-in-fact agreement not to terminate Plaintiff without cause. However, there is no evidence that the Addendum was legally executed, and therefore neither party is bound by its term, express or implied. Finally, because Plaintiff is unable to prove any other cause of action, Defendants are entitled to judgment on the unfair business practices claim. (Continued to Part 2)

rulings.Case Title: CENTRAL GARDENS, INC. VS. ROBIN DEWAR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, December 19, 2014, Line 3: DEFENDANTS ROBIN DEWAR and DENNIS O'LEARY'S Motion For Protective Order. Pro Tem Judge Jeff Wohl, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: AHMED ABUBAKER et al VS. HORIZON LINES, LLC A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compwl And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, December 19, 2014, Line 6: DEFENDANT HORIZON LINES, LLC'S, a Delaware limited liability corporation, Motion To Compel And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Jeff Wohl, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge.
